rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are declared or visible in the current scope before use.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning expression types with declared helper types or feature types.
Remove invalid guarantee or assign clauses that reference unresolved identifiers or have <error> types.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions to avoid 'Couldn't resolve reference' errors.
Ensure all AGREE expressions have consistent types to prevent 'left and right sides of binary expression are of type' errors.
Validate that every named thing in a guarantee or assign statement has a valid type before the validator processes it.
When encountering '<error>' types, trace back to the first unresolved identifier and correct the root cause.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE requires consistent types in binary expressions; use eq helpers to cast or align types when needed.
Use 'eq' declarations for intermediate values and 'assign' for output port assignments in implementation scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to fix unresolved references; instead, correct the references or remove invalid clauses.
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope; use only assign for output port assignments.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
